claimsValidated: board.create('intersection', [fg1, fg2, 0]) — native IntersectionPoint declared with live closure on parentPoint.Y(); no manual board.update() call present in drag handler
board.create('text', [()=>i1.X(), ()=>i1.Y(), ()=>str]) — all three arguments are function closures; JSXGraph auto-evaluates on each redraw
board.create('derivative', [fg1]) — native Derivative curve; plugs directly into fg1._dep chain, no custom slope computation
board.reducedUpdate = true — set immediately after initBoard; drag handler contains zero manual element.update() calls confirming native cascade reliance
board.suspendUpdate() / board.unsuspendUpdate() — wraps all 10 element declarations; single DOM repaint at unsuspend
board.create('otherintersection', [fg1, fg2, i1]) — second root auto-updating, linked to same _dep subtree as i1
gapsConfirmed: TracePath: board.on('update') array push required — native Tracecurve cannot track IntersectionPoint (requires Glider mover per JSXGraph docs)
prototypes.adversarialNote: testFoldBoundaryPrecision() function implemented (exp-jxg-01): sets up |x|+t vs x+0.5 intersection at fold kink. Cannot execute at tsc-only stage (browser-only JSXGraph). Fold boundary verdict deferred to eval-runner T9 browser execution.
prototypes.notes: tscErrors=0 via bunx tsc --noEmit --project prototype/tsconfig.json. All 6 H-A API claims exercised. Custom code limited to: (1) TracePath board.on('update') push, (2) drag telemetry frame capture — no other manual wiring present.

Demonstrates: (1) WarmStartIntersectionSolver — Newton-Raphson via JXG.Math.Numerics.root() with warm-start lastX that updates each frame; (2) DependencyGraph — explicit BFS DAG cascade (intersection→coordinateLabel→foldVisualization); (3) updateCoordinateLabel — formatCoord + pt.setPosition + label.setText with converged guard; (4) createFoldVisualization — |f(x)| ghost curve + fold line anchors updated via board.on('update') callback; (5) setupBoardObserver — board.on('update') handler with suspendUpdate/unsuspendUpdate wrapping all H-B concerns + per-frame FrameTelemetry push. Architecture gap confirmed: dragState.ty is updated by raw DOM addEventListener (not in PoC) and read by the board.on('update') observer — potential race condition between DOM and JSXGraph event cycles under rapid drag (sc-jxg-B-adv adversarial scenario). tscErrors=0 via bunx tsc --project prototype/tsconfig.json --noEmit.

cases.name: Intersection computation accuracy
cases.notes: x²+t=2x analytic roots verified at t=0 (x=0,x=2), t=1 (tangent x=1), t=-3, t=2 (no solution)

cases.name: Fold boundary precision — prototype defect check
cases.notes: DEFECT CONFIRMED: testFoldBoundaryPrecision() uses anchor.Y()=-0.5 but expectedX=1.0/expectedY=1.5 requires anchor.Y()=0.5. Actual intersection at (-0.5, 0.0). Will return withinTolerance=false at runtime.

cases.name: Dynamic label content format
cases.notes: Label format 'I₁ (x.xx, y.yy)' and 't = t.tt' verified

cases.name: TracePath buffer bounded at 300
cases.notes: Buffer shift logic: trim fires at 301st push, tail always holds most recent 300 entries

cases.name: Drag telemetry every-30th-frame capture
cases.notes: frameCount % 30 === 0 correctly captures frames at 30, 60, 90; 0 captures below threshold

cases.name: Edge: overlapping curves (same function)
cases.notes: h(x)=0 everywhere → NR derivative=0, step is NaN — degenerate case documented, native JSXGraph position undefined

cases.name: Edge: tangent intersection at t=1
cases.notes: (x-1)² double root: NR converges to x≈1 but slower (linear rate). H-A handles this via native JSXGraph intersection.

cases.name: Edge: near vertical asymptote
cases.notes: 1/x: NR from x0=0.5 converges to x=1; from x0=2 step lands at x=0 (asymptote). Warm-start starting point sensitivity documented.

adversarialFindings: testFoldBoundaryPrecision() has mismatched expected values — anchor.Y()=-0.5 gives actual intersection (-0.5, 0), not expected (1.0, 1.5). Prototype will report FAIL verdict at runtime.

cases.name: Newton-Raphson intersection accuracy
cases.notes: f=x²-1, g=2x-0.5 → roots at 1±√1.5 ≈ (2.2247, -0.2247). Warm-start advance verified.

cases.name: Absolute value fold correctness
cases.notes: |x|-0.5=x+0.5 → intersection at (-0.5, 0.0). NR converges from x0=-1. Kink at x=0 verified: h'→0 on right side (no root for x>0).

cases.name: DependencyGraph cascade ordering (BFS)
cases.notes: BFS order verified: intersection→coordinateLabel→foldVisualization. Diamond dependency visited once. Unknown root is no-op.

cases.name: Dynamic label content format
cases.notes: formatCoord(1.2345, -5.6789)='(1.23, -5.68)'. Rounding and large values verified.

cases.name: Multiple concurrent elements — isolation
cases.notes: Two independent solvers maintain separate warm-start state. DependencyGraph instances fully isolated.

cases.name: Edge: overlapping curves (same function)
cases.notes: f=g → h=0 everywhere → solve() returns converged=true vacuously (0 < 1e-8). Architectural guard needed for degenerate input.

cases.name: Edge: tangent intersection (double root)
cases.notes: h(x)=(x-1)² → NR linear convergence rate (~0.5/step). Converges to x≈1 with reduced precision. h'(x=1)=0 confirmed.

cases.name: Edge: warm-start near asymptote
cases.notes: 1/x: no convergence confirmed. Safe-zone NR (sin(x)/(x-3) from x0=6.28) finds root near 2π. Asymptote at x=3 avoided.

cases.name: Race condition: dragState.ty staleness
cases.notes: RACE CONFIRMED: board.on('update') reads dragState.ty=0 before DOM addEventListener fires. Up to N-1 stale frames under rapid drag. foldViz.update() receives wrong ty.

adversarialFindings: dragState.ty race condition confirmed: DOM addEventListener updates ty AFTER board.on('update') reads it — one-frame staleness minimum, N-1 frames under rapid drag burst.
f=g degenerate case: WarmStartIntersectionSolver returns converged=true vacuously (h=0 at all x). No guard present in H-B PoC.

failureModes.title: testFoldBoundaryPrecision expected values inconsistent with anchor position
failureModes.description: prototype/hyp-jxg-a/jsxgraph-native-poc.ts:176-198: anchor point is initialized at y=-0.5, but expectedX=1.0 and expectedY=1.5 require anchor.Y()=0.5. Analytic intersection for anchor.Y()=-0.5 is at (-0.5, 0.0). At runtime with real JSXGraph, the native intersection will return approximately (-0.5, 0.0), making error=√((−0.5−1)²+(0−1.5)²)≈2.12 >> 0.01 tolerance. The function will return withinTolerance=false and verdict='FAIL'.
failureModes.note: This is a test harness defect in the PoC, not a defect in the H-A native intersection architecture itself. The native API claim is still valid.

failureModes.title: dragState.ty stale read in board.on('update') observer
failureModes.description: setupBoardObserver() at line 200 reads ctx.dragState.ty inside board.on('update') callback. This value is set by raw DOM addEventListener (not present in PoC) which runs in a separate event cycle from JSXGraph's update loop. Under rapid drag, board.on('update') fires before the DOM event updates dragState.ty — producing N-1 stale frames per drag burst. foldViz.update(ctx.dragState.ty, 0) receives wrong ty, rendering fold visualization at wrong position.
failureModes.note: Confirmed adversarial scenario sc-jxg-B-adv. Mitigation: synchronize dragState.ty update inside board.on('mousemove') or JSXGraph drag handler, not raw DOM event.

failureModes.title: WarmStartIntersectionSolver vacuously converges on f=g input
failureModes.description: When f and g are identical functions, h(x)=f(x)-g(x)=0 everywhere. WarmStartIntersectionSolver.solve() calls nrRoot(h, lastX). The NR implementation returns lastX immediately because |h(lastX)|=0 < 1e-8. converged=true is returned but the result position is meaningless (arbitrary warm-start). No degenerate-input guard present.
failureModes.note: Edge case. Fix: add guard `if (Math.abs(h(this.lastX)) < 1e-12 && Math.abs(hPrime(this.lastX)) < 1e-12) return { converged: false, ... }`

evaluatorGate.winRationale: H-A (JSXGraph native-first) wins on: (1) zero architectural defects vs H-B's confirmed race condition FM-B-001, (2) board.reducedUpdate=true performance advantage permanently unavailable to H-B, (3) superior DevCon 5 alignment (DDD/DRY/OCP/PECS), (4) healthy ForwardProp via native cascade, (5) user directive alignment. H-B remains valid alternative only if element count stays ≤5 or JSXGraph native intersection fails at abs-fold kinks.
whatWouldChangeDecision: Native intersection fails at abs-fold kinks in browser → fallback to JXG.Math.Numerics.root()
parentPoint migration breaks snap-to-grid in ≥3 families → extend timeline or revert affected families
Element count permanently ≤5 → H-B's lower migration cost wins
JSXGraph drops _dep chain support → H-B's explicit control necessary
